channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Report: Tencent mulling exits from multiple Japanese studios
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 23, 2026

1 Min Read

Chinese tech and video game conglomerate Tencent is reportedly negotiating exits from several of its studio investments in Japan.

People with knowledge of the matter told Bloomberg the company is reassessing its global portfolio and is prepared to sell back minority stakes in several Japanese studios to their respective management teams—even if that means taking a financial hit by selling at a loss.

Tencent is purportedly eager to refocus its portfolio around user-generated content platforms and experiences such as Minecraft and Roblox.

Since 2020, the company has invested in notable Japanese game companies such as Marvelous, Wake Up, PlatinumGames Inc, and FromSoftware.

Sources have told Bloomberg that Tencent's investments in the latter two companies—known for working on Ninja Gaiden and Elden Ring, respectively—are not on the chopping block. The fate of others, however, is less certain. 

When approached for comment by Bloomberg, a Tencent spokesperson said video games remain core to its business. “We remain fully committed to working with our investees and maintaining our strong presence in the Japanese game market over the long term," they added. 

Related:Valve is making it easier to build your own Steam Machine

A Marvelous spokesperson declined to comment.

The news comes shortly after French studio Don't Nod confirmed Tencent has decided against injecting more cash into the company, leaving the Life is Strange developer facing an uncertain future. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/report-tencent-mulling-exits-from-multiple-japanese-studios
